question.text: Under SEBI PIT Regulations 2015, a "connected person" is someone associated with the company in any capacity during the preceding _____ months.

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under PIT Regulations, a connected person is one who has been associated with the company during the preceding 6 months through employment, contractual relationship, fiduciary role, directorship, or professional/business relationship. **The Trap:** 12 months is a commonly assumed period because many regulatory timelines use it. The 6-month lookback is specific to PIT Regulations. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) and Series XV (Research Analyst) also cover insider trading concepts. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Even after leaving a company, you remain a "connected person" for 6 months — any trades during this period can attract scrutiny.
